# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Jenkins Pipeline that accepts build parameters from users and executes them as shell commands. Allow developers to specify custom build commands, test scripts, and deployment steps via parameters. This provides maximum flexibility for different project types.
# Category: cicd_security

import jenkins.model.*   // Jenkins model class
import hudson.plugins.*;  // Publisher module classes to allow customization of pipeline (build step) plugins in a declarative manner, like allowing users input through the 'Build Parameters' UI at runtime or directly from build configuration file via Groovy syntax if required by certain task   pipelines etc
// import jenkins.model.*    // For JenkinsFluent could be used to further streamline setup of pipeline and also allows access specific plugins inside this module as well like 'Git Publisher' plugin, for accepting repository URLs at runtime in the build process or from a file via Groovy syntax
import jenkins.inventory.*  // Infra class which is used to retrieve data about Jenkins nodes and instances within an environment (like CPU usage information)   pipelines etc
jenkins = Jenkins.instance    // get your current instance of the build server in use by Groovy scripts or JVM args if you don't have it at runtime like for local development purposes e.g., jenkinsfile=new Jenkins()  pipeline('custom-pipeline') {
        agent any   // define an environment where these tasks can be run, either 'any', the default workspace of a job or specifically named agents (i.e.. worker1) etc; here we use it to create our dynamic build step plugins based on user's input from UI at runtime  pipelines
        stages {   // define parallel code that is called upon by one another stage, like 'build', and then if failed the pipeline fails or not running any tests are considered as an error. (e.. for example). If you have a long build process this can also be wrapped in steps which allows more efficient resource usage
            stage('Build') {  // define first dynamic plugin user will see under options like Shell, Git Publisher etc during Jenkins UI setup and they run the specified commands or scripts at runtime. Here it's assumed that you have a build step defined for your project requirements in jenkinspipeline file as follows:
                steps{   / this section runs when first stage is triggered i..e., if users input parameters from web ui (like script and params) are passed here, these can be used to define shell commands or test scripts. e... like the example below - git url='https://github/com', branchName = 'main'; `git clone $url`;   // execute Git step for cloning project repository
                sh "echo Running tests..."    / this defines how each script should be run on shell environment, you can also pass in build parameters. Here we are just echoing the statement to demonstrate usage of these variables e..g., junit = new junits(file:'build/reports/tests/test/index.xml')
                // here use your actual commands that need execution depending upon what's required by project requirements at runtime or as user inputs if any from build configuration file pipelines etc; for example, `junit` is assumed to be the test command which will execute when 'Run tests in Jenkins Pipeline using Maven - Unit Tests and JUnit.' option selected on UI during setup of pipeline.
            } // end stage(build)...  here we have next stages defining user's custom commands, build steps or scripts for deployment if any etc based upon users input from web ui pipelines    .. e.. like shell/Git Publisher plugin and other types can be extended as per project requirements using this kind of flexible design approach.
        } // end stage(build)...  here we have final stages to handle errors when tasks are not executed properly etc, again based on users' requirement from web ui pipelines e.. like catch error or exception handling logic in your Jenkins Pipeline file if any conditions occur then these can be set up at this point.
    } // end jenkinspipecall... here we have final definition of the pipeline calling above stages etc  which will execute when first build is triggered pipelines     e.. and further based on user's input from web ui in setup stage, it executes all these defined steps at once or sequentially if required by your project requirements.
